indication: New diagnosis of atrial fibrillation, recent initiation of anticoagulation, baseline evaluation of intracranial and extracranial vasculature
technique: 3D time-of-flight MRA of the circle of Willis and 2D time-of-flight MRA of the neck vessels were performed without contrast administration due to chronic kidney disease.

intracranialArteries.ica: Bilateral intracranial internal carotid arteries are patent with normal caliber and flow signal.
intracranialArteries.mca: Bilateral middle cerebral arteries and their major branches are patent. No stenosis or occlusion.
intracranialArteries.aca: Bilateral anterior cerebral arteries are patent. Normal A1 segments.
intracranialArteries.pca: Bilateral posterior cerebral arteries are patent. Normal caliber.
intracranialArteries.vertebrobasilar: Basilar artery is patent with normal caliber. Bilateral vertebral arteries show normal intracranial flow.
intracranialArteries.circleOfWillis: Complete circle of Willis. No intracranial aneurysm. No arteriovenous malformation.

extracranialArteries.commonCarotid: Bilateral common carotid arteries are patent with normal flow signal.
extracranialArteries.internalCarotid: Mild signal heterogeneity at the right carotid bifurcation suggesting mild atherosclerotic disease, without significant stenosis. Left ICA is patent with minimal irregularity.
extracranialArteries.externalCarotid: Bilateral external carotid arteries are patent.
extracranialArteries.vertebral: Bilateral vertebral arteries are patent with normal caliber and antegrade flow. Codominant. No stenosis or dissection.

findings.incidentalFindings: Mild periventricular white matter signal changes on localizer images consistent with early chronic microvascular disease.

impression: No significant intracranial arterial stenosis, occlusion, or aneurysm.
Mild atherosclerotic changes at the right carotid bifurcation without hemodynamically significant stenosis.
Patent bilateral vertebral arteries without stenosis.
Early chronic microvascular white matter changes noted incidentally.
recommendations: Continue anticoagulation for stroke prevention in atrial fibrillation
Consider follow-up MRA in 1-2 years to assess for progression
comparison: No prior MRA available for comparison.
